question: Is the image laid out on a horizontal white background with two distinct rows (Upper ~60% height, Lower ~40% height)?
Are the upper circuit module and lower chart modules framed with thin gray borders?
Does the upper circuit schematic contain exactly two op-amps labeled U1 and U2, drawn as triangle symbols with '+' and '-' inputs marked?
Are there exactly six resistors labeled R1-R6 drawn using zigzag symbols?
Are there exactly three capacitors labeled C1-C3 drawn using parallel plate symbols?
Does the circuit include an input source labeled VIN (circular symbol) and an output labeled VOUT (test point dot)?
Are power rails labeled +V and -V connected to the op-amps, and are standard ground symbols used throughout?
Are the key nodes labeled N1 through N6 present in the circuit?
Is all circuit wiring drawn using only horizontal and vertical lines (right angles) with no diagonal lines?
Is there a clearly drawn feedback network connecting the output back to the inverting (-) input?
Does the lower-left area (approx. 2/3 width) contain a Bode plot with two vertically stacked subplots sharing a logarithmic frequency axis?
Does the upper Bode subplot show a MAG(dB) curve with a -40 dB/dec downward trend (two-corner rolloff)?
Does the lower Bode subplot show a PHASE(deg) curve decreasing from approximately 0 to -180 degrees?
Are vertical dashed lines labeled f1 and f2 present across the Bode plots?
Does the lower-right area (approx. 1/3 width) contain a Step Response plot with axes labeled 't' and 'Vout'?
In the Step Response plot, is the input Vin shown as a gray square wave and the output as a blue rising curve with overshoot?
Are horizontal dashed lines labeled 'P' (peak) and 'SS' (steady-state) marked on the Step Response plot?
Do all chart axes include visible tick marks?
Is the color palette strictly limited to black, gray, and blue (on white background)?
Is all text contained in the image composed of Latin letters and numbers only?
